description: Initialize Tauri v2 + Svelte 5 project with TailwindCSS v4
steps: Create Tauri v2 project with Svelte frontend using `bun create tauri-app` or manual setup
Configure Svelte 5 with TypeScript strict mode
Install and configure TailwindCSS v4 with @tailwindcss/vite
Verify `cargo tauri dev` launches the app window with a Hello World page
Verify hot-reload works for both Svelte and Rust changes
Ensure `cargo check` and `cargo clippy` pass with no warnings

description: Build the main app shell layout with sidebar, header, and content area
steps: Create a top header bar with app name 'Aiffer', folder path display area, and a 'Submit Review' button placeholder
Create a left sidebar panel (resizable, ~250px default width) for the file tree
Create a main content area that fills remaining space for the diff viewer
Add a collapsible right panel placeholder for AI response
Apply GitHub-inspired styling: dark header, white/neutral content background, subtle borders
Verify layout is responsive and panels don't overflow

description: Implement git diff reading in Rust using git2 crate
steps: Add git2, serde, serde_json, thiserror, chrono, uuid dependencies to Cargo.toml
Create src-tauri/src/models/ module with DiffFile, DiffHunk, DiffLine, FileStatus structs (all Serialize/Deserialize)
Create src-tauri/src/git/ module with function to open a git repo from a given path
Implement function to read unstaged diff (working directory vs index)
Implement function to read staged diff (index vs HEAD)
Parse raw diff into structured DiffFile → DiffHunk → DiffLine hierarchy
Write unit tests: test parsing a known diff produces correct structure
Ensure cargo test passes

description: Create Tauri IPC commands to expose git diff data to frontend
steps: Create src-tauri/src/commands/ module
Implement #[tauri::command] get_diff(path: String, diff_type: String) that returns Vec<DiffFile>
Implement #[tauri::command] get_file_status(path: String) that returns list of changed files with their statuses
Register commands in Tauri builder (main.rs invoke_handler)
Test commands via frontend console: invoke('get_diff', { path: '.', diffType: 'unstaged' })
Handle errors gracefully — return user-friendly error messages for non-git directories

description: Build the file tree sidebar showing changed files with status indicators
steps: Create FileTree.svelte component that calls get_file_status Tauri command on mount
Display files in a vertical list with file path and status icon (A=green, M=yellow, D=red, R=blue)
Show diff stats per file (+N -M) in muted text next to each file
Highlight the currently selected file
Clicking a file scrolls the main diff viewer to that file's section
Add a folder open button in the header that triggers Tauri file dialog to select a folder
Add a refresh button to re-read diffs
Show overall diff summary at the top (total files changed, additions, deletions)

description: Build the unified diff viewer displaying file diffs with line numbers
steps: Create DiffViewer.svelte component that receives diff data and renders all files
Create DiffFileSection.svelte — collapsible section per file with header (path, status badge, stats)
Create DiffHunk.svelte — renders a hunk with @@ header line in blue/purple
Create DiffLine.svelte — renders a single line with old/new line numbers in the gutter
Apply GitHub diff colors: green background (#e6ffec) for additions, red (#ffebe9) for deletions, white for context
Use monospace font for diff content
Verify multi-file diffs render correctly with proper line numbering
Verify file sections can be collapsed/expanded

description: Add hover effects, line highlighting, and expand/collapse to diff viewer
steps: Add hover highlight on diff lines (subtle background change on mouseover)
Show a '+' icon in the gutter on line hover (comment trigger button)
Add click-to-select line highlighting (like GitHub's yellow highlight when clicking line numbers)
Add 'Expand all' / 'Collapse all' buttons for file sections in the header
Add smooth scroll-to-file when clicking file in sidebar
Ensure line numbers are properly aligned and never wrap

description: Implement comment data model and state management in Svelte stores
steps: Define TypeScript types: Comment, CommentWithContext in src/lib/types/
Create a Svelte store (comments.ts) that holds Map<string, Comment[]> keyed by 'filepath:lineno'
Implement addComment(filePath, lineNumber, lineType, body) action
Implement removeComment(commentId) action
Implement editComment(commentId, newBody) action
Implement getCommentsForLine(filePath, lineNumber) derived getter
Implement getAllComments() that returns a flat sorted list of all comments
Write basic store tests or verify via manual testing

description: Build the inline comment form that appears when clicking the '+' icon on a diff line
steps: Create CommentForm.svelte — a form that appears below the target diff line
Form includes: textarea for comment body, 'Add Comment' submit button, 'Cancel' button
Clicking the '+' gutter icon opens the form below that line (pushes content down)
Pressing Cmd+Enter submits the comment
Pressing Escape cancels and closes the form
After submitting, the form closes and the comment appears as a CommentThread
Style to match GitHub: white card with subtle border, rounded corners, shadow

description: Build comment thread display showing existing comments on diff lines
steps: Create CommentThread.svelte — renders below a diff line when comments exist
Display each comment with: avatar placeholder, timestamp, comment body
Add 'Edit' button that turns the comment body into an editable textarea
Add 'Delete' button with confirmation
Show a comment count badge on the gutter for lines that have comments
Comments collapse by default if there are more than 3; show 'Show N more' link
Style to match GitHub comment cards: border-left accent, subtle background

description: Build the review summary panel showing all comments collected
steps: Create ReviewSummary.svelte — a panel showing all comments grouped by file
Each entry shows: file path, line number, comment body, and a snippet of the code line
Clicking an entry scrolls to that comment in the diff viewer
Show total comment count in the header
Add 'Submit Review' button that triggers AI submission
Add 'Clear All' button to remove all comments (with confirmation dialog)
Panel can be toggled via a button in the top header bar

description: Implement comment collection and serialization for AI agent payload
steps: Create ReviewPayload struct in Rust models: comments with context, diff summary, file list
Create CommentWithContext struct: comment fields + surrounding code lines (3 lines above and below)
Implement Tauri command build_review_payload(path, comments_json) that enriches comments with code context
The payload should include: the comment body, the exact line commented on, surrounding context lines, file path, and line number
Serialize the payload to a clean, readable string format suitable for LLM consumption
Write unit test: given sample comments and diff, verify payload structure

description: Implement AI agent communication — send review payload and receive response
steps: Create src-tauri/src/ai/ module for AI agent communication
Implement a generic AI client trait that can be backed by different providers
Implement OpenAI-compatible API client (works with OpenAI, Anthropic via proxy, local models)
Create Tauri command submit_review(payload_json, api_config) that sends to AI and returns response
API config includes: endpoint URL, API key, model name
Build a system prompt that instructs the AI to interpret review comments and explain what changes the user wants
Handle streaming responses if possible, otherwise return full response
Handle API errors gracefully with user-friendly messages

description: Build the AI response display panel
steps: Create AiResponsePanel.svelte — right panel that shows AI agent's response
Display response with basic markdown rendering (bold, italic, code blocks, lists)
Show a loading spinner while waiting for AI response
Show error state if AI request fails with retry button
Add 'Copy to Clipboard' button for the AI response
Panel slides in from right when AI response is received
Add close button to dismiss the panel

description: Build settings panel for AI configuration and app preferences
steps: Create Settings.svelte — modal or slide-over panel for app settings
Add AI Configuration section: API endpoint URL, API key (masked input), model name
Add Diff Options section: toggle staged/unstaged diff, number of context lines
Persist settings using Tauri's fs or store plugin (save to local config file)
Load settings on app startup
Add a gear icon in the header that opens settings
Validate API key format and endpoint URL before saving

description: Add toggle between staged, unstaged, and commit-range diffs
steps: Add a diff type selector in the header: 'Unstaged Changes', 'Staged Changes', 'Between Commits'
Implement Rust backend support for comparing two arbitrary commits
When 'Between Commits' is selected, show two commit selector dropdowns (from/to)
Switching diff type re-fetches and re-renders the diff
Preserve comments when switching diff types (warn user if comments may become invalid)
Show the current diff type prominently in the header

description: Add keyboard shortcuts for common actions
steps: Implement keyboard shortcut handler at the app level
Add shortcuts: 'n' = next file, 'p' = previous file in diff viewer
Add shortcuts: 'j' = next hunk, 'k' = previous hunk within current file
Add shortcut: 'c' = open comment form on currently highlighted line
Add shortcut: 'Cmd+Enter' = submit review
Add shortcut: 'Cmd+Shift+R' = refresh diffs
Add shortcut: '?' = show keyboard shortcut help overlay
Ensure shortcuts don't fire when typing in text inputs

description: Add syntax highlighting for diff content based on file type
steps: Integrate a lightweight syntax highlighting library (e.g., Prism.js or highlight.js via CDN or bundled)
Detect file language from file extension
Apply syntax highlighting to diff line content while preserving diff coloring
Support at minimum: JavaScript, TypeScript, Rust, Python, Go, HTML, CSS, JSON, YAML, Markdown
Ensure highlighting doesn't break on partial lines (additions/deletions may be incomplete)
Test with various file types to ensure correct coloring

description: Implement dark and light theme toggle matching GitHub's theme
steps: Define CSS custom properties for both light and dark themes
Light theme: white background, dark text, GitHub-classic diff colors
Dark theme: #0d1117 background, #e6edf3 text, dark-mode diff colors (#1a4721 additions, #67060c deletions)
Add theme toggle button in the header (sun/moon icon)
Persist theme preference in settings
Apply theme class to the root element
Verify all components render correctly in both themes

description: Implement folder opening via file dialog and drag-and-drop
steps: Use Tauri dialog plugin to open a native folder picker
After folder is selected, validate it's a git repository
If not a git repo, show an error message suggesting to initialize one
Support drag-and-drop a folder onto the app window to open it
Update the header to show the current folder name and path
Remember last opened folder and offer to reopen on next launch
Support opening via CLI argument: `aiffer /path/to/repo`

description: Add comprehensive error handling and loading states throughout the app
steps: Add loading skeletons for diff viewer while data is loading
Add empty state when no diffs are found ('No changes detected' with icon)
Add error boundary at the app level that catches and displays unhandled errors
Add toast/notification system for non-blocking messages (e.g., 'Settings saved', 'Comment added')
Handle git errors: not a repo, corrupted repo, permission denied
Handle network errors for AI API calls with clear user feedback
Ensure all error messages are actionable (tell user what to do)

description: Create end-to-end smoke tests for core user flow
steps: Set up a test git repo fixture with known file changes
Test: app launches and displays diff viewer
Test: changing a file and refreshing shows the diff correctly
Test: clicking a diff line opens comment form
Test: submitting a comment shows it in the thread and review summary
Test: clicking 'Submit Review' collects all comments (mock AI response)
Document how to run tests in CLAUDE.md
